steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a clear subject (‘the International Criminal Police Organization (Interpol)’) and an object (‘offices in more than 200 cities’). The blank must be filled by a main verb that agrees with the singular subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'to oversee': infinitive — cannot serve as the main verb of the sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'oversees': third-person singular present tense — matches the singular subject ‘Interpol’ and functions as a proper main verb. ✓
  • Choice C — 'overseeing': present participle — cannot function as a standalone main verb without an auxiliary. ✗
  • Choice D — 'having overseen': perfect participle — a non-finite form that cannot serve as a main verb. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
